revision.text:
'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine ranking system|ja|社格|12=draft|12=draft}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft|12=draft}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft|12=draft}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|前田利家|12=draft|12=draft}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine|ja|尾山神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|lt=Takami Musubi|lt_ja=高皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manor|ja|荘園|12=draft|12=draft}}) of {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Himemiko|ja|比売神|lt=Hime-no-kami|lt_ja=比咩神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Take-Mikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|lt_ja=武甕槌大神|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|Minato Ward, Tokyo|ja|港区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=draft|12=draft}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|ja|中原中也|12=draft|12=draft}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Muro Saisei|ja|室生犀星|12=draft|12=draft}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|ja|永仁|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=draft|12=draft}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikko-ikki|ja|一向一揆|12=draft|12=draft}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|12=draft|12=draft}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=draft|12=draft}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別尊|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=気長足姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0324/ Utasu Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|12=draft|12=draft}} * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0107/ Kosaka Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|12=draft|12=draft}} * [https://www.shinmeiguu-kanazawa.jp/ Shinmeigu Shrine] * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0121/ Tsubakihara Tenmangu] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|12=draft|12=draft}} * [http://www.yasue-hachimangu.or.jp/ Yasue Hachimangu / Kanazawa Suitengū]
{{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories|
}}

{{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories|
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanazawa]] [[Category:History of Kanazawa]] [[Category:Shinmei Shrines]] [[Category:Tenmangu Shrines]] [[Category:Hachimangu Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengū Shrines]] [[Category:Japan's Famous Five]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }}
[[ja:金沢五社]] [[sn:Kanazawa Gosha]]

'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} and {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|前田利家}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine|ja|尾山神社}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|lt=Takami Musubi|lt_ja=高皇産霊神}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manor|ja|荘園}}) of {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himemiko|ja|比売神|lt=Hime-no-kami|lt_ja=比咩神}}, and {{ill|Take-Mikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|lt_ja=武甕槌大神}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|Minato Ward, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|ja|中原中也}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Muro Saisei|ja|室生犀星}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|ja|永仁}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikko-ikki|ja|一向一揆}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別尊}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=気長足姫尊}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0324/ Utasu Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁}} * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0107/ Kosaka Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁}} * [https://www.shinmeiguu-kanazawa.jp/ Shinmeigu Shrine] * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0121/ Tsubakihara Tenmangu] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁}} * [http://www.yasue-hachimangu.or.jp/ Yasue Hachimangu / Kanazawa Suitengū]

revision.comment: 54 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金沢五社 up to rev 99296760
revision.text:
'''金沢五社'''（'''かなざわごしゃ'''）は、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]に所在する[[神社]]のうち、[[江戸時代]]から鎮座する[[宇多須神社]]、小坂神社、神明宮、椿原天満宮、安江八幡宮の[[吉田神道]]の五つの神社の総称である。
五社ともに近代の[[社格]]制度では県社に列格された。この五社すべてを参拝することを「五社参り」といい、盛んに行われた。
== 宇多須神社 == [[ファイル:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|宇多須神社]] '''[[宇多須神社]]'''（うたすじんじゃ）は、[[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）の創建。[[1599年]]（[[慶長]]4年）に[[前田利家]]を密かに祀り、藩士が禄高に応じて祭祀料を負担した。[[尾山神社]]の創建に伴い元の祭神である[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]を遷座した。
所在地：金沢市東山一丁目30番8号
== 小坂神社 == [[ファイル:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|小坂神社]] '''小坂神社'''（こさかじんじゃ）は、[[717年]]（養老元年)の創建。[[春日大社]]の[[荘園 (日本)|荘園]]だった土地で<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html 小坂神社本殿] - 金沢市</ref>、この神社も春日大社の末社にあたり「春日さん」として親しまれる。主祭神は[[アメノコヤネ|天児屋根命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[比売神|比咩神]]、[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌大神]]。
所在地：金沢市山の上町42番1号
== 神明宮 == '''神明宮'''（しんめいぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[芝大門]]鎮座の[[芝大神宮]]（芝神明宮）、東岩倉神明（[[京都]]）などと並んで、[[神明神社|日本七神明]]の一つ。春と秋に行われる「[[あぶり餅|あぶりもち]]神事」で知られる<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>。[[中原中也]]の[[詩]]『サーカス』は、幼年期にこの神社の境内で見た[[サーカス]]を書いたもの。また、[[室生犀星]]の生家に近く[[詩人]]に縁のある神社である。主祭神は[[アマテラス|天照皇大神]]、[[豊受姫大神]]。
所在地：金沢市野町二丁目1番8号
== 椿原天満宮 == [[ファイル:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|椿原天満宮]] '''椿原天満宮'''（つばきはらてんまんぐう）は、[[1297年]]（[[永仁]]5年）に[[守護]]富樫義親によって創建された[[天満宮]]のひとつ。[[江戸時代]]の[[寛永]]年間に現在の地に移る。[[一向一揆]]の際に須崎兵庫が陣取った椿原砦の址が残る。現在では度々[[拝殿]]で[[室内楽]]、[[フォークソング|フォーク]]・[[ロック (音楽)|ロック]]・[[ブルース]][[バンド (音楽)|バンド]]などの[[コンサート]]が行われ好評。主祭神は[[菅原道真]]。
所在地：金沢市天神町一丁目1番13号
== 安江八幡宮 == [[ファイル:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|安江八幡宮]] '''安江八幡宮'''（やすえはちまんぐう）は、[[939年]]（[[天慶]]2年）の創建。'''金沢水天宮'''の別名があり、石川県内で唯一の[[水天宮]]が祀っている。 [[ファイル:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|安江八幡宮の「[[加賀八幡起上り]]」の像]] [[郷土玩具]]「加賀八幡起上がり」はこの神社に奉納されたのが起源で、石川県の[[マスコット]]キャラクター「[[ひゃくまんさん]]」はこれをモチーフとしている。主祭神は[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]、[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]]。
所在地：金沢市此花町11番27号
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0324/ 宇多須神社] - 石川県神社庁 * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0107/ 小坂神社] - 石川県神社庁 * [https://www.shinmeiguu-kanazawa.jp/ 神明宮] * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0121/ 椿原天満宮] - 石川県神社庁 * [http://www.yasue-hachimangu.or.jp/ 安江八幡宮・金沢水天宮]
{{デフォルトソート:かなさわこしや}} [[Category:石川県の神社|*かなさわこしや]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社|*かなさわこしや]] [[Category:金沢市の建築物|*]] [[Category:金沢市の歴史]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:水天宮]] [[Category:日本の名数5]]

Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|Minato Ward, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|ja|中原中也}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Muro Saisei|ja|室生犀星}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|ja|永仁}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikko-ikki|ja|一向一揆}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別尊}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=気長足姫尊}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

[[ja:金沢五社]] [[sn:Kanazawa Gosha]] {{translated page|ja|金沢五社|version=99296760|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=draft|12=draft}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft|12=draft}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft|12=draft}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|前田利家|12=draft|12=draft}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine|ja|尾山神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|lt=Takami Musubi|lt_ja=高皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manor|ja|荘園|12=draft|12=draft}}) of {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Himemiko|ja|比売神|lt=Hime-no-kami|lt_ja=比咩神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Take-Mikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|lt_ja=武甕槌大神|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|Minato Ward, Tokyo|ja|港区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=draft|12=draft}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|ja|中原中也|12=draft|12=draft}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Muro Saisei|ja|室生犀星|12=draft|12=draft}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8

It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikko-ikki|ja|一向一揆|12=draft|12=draft}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13

The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Himemiko|ja|比売神|lt=Hime-no-kami|lt_ja=比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himemiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Take-Mikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|lt_ja=武甕槌大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-Mikazuchi|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|Minato Ward, Tokyo|ja|港区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Ward, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|ja|中原中也|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūya Nakahara|qq=}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Muro Saisei|ja|室生犀星|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muro Saisei|qq=}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|ja|永仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Einin|qq=}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikko-ikki|ja|一向一揆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikko-ikki|qq=}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haiden|qq=}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkei|qq=}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengū Shrine|qq=}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Emperor|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

[[ja:金沢五社]] [[sn:Kanazawa Gosha]]

'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazawa City|qq=}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|qq=}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|前田利家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeda Toshiie|qq=}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine|ja|尾山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|lt=Takami Musubi|lt_ja=高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manor|ja|荘園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manor|qq=}}) of {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Taisha|qq=}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Himemiko|ja|比売神|lt=Hime-no-kami|lt_ja=比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himemiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Take-Mikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|lt_ja=武甕槌大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-Mikazuchi|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|Minato Ward, Tokyo|ja|港区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Ward, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|ja|中原中也|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūya Nakahara|qq=}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Muro Saisei|ja|室生犀星|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muro Saisei|qq=}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|ja|永仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Einin|qq=}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikko-ikki|ja|一向一揆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikko-ikki|qq=}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haiden|qq=}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkei|qq=}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengū Shrine|qq=}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Emperor|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|ja|永仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Einin|qq=}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikko-ikki|ja|一向一揆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikko-ikki|qq=}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haiden|qq=}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkei|qq=}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengū Shrine|qq=}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Emperor|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

[[ja:金沢五社]] [[sn:Kanazawa Gosha]]

'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazawa City|qq=}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|qq=}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|前田利家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeda Toshiie|qq=}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine|ja|尾山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|lt=Takami Musubi|lt_ja=高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manor|ja|荘園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manor|qq=}}) of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Taisha|qq=}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Himemiko|ja|比売神|lt=Hime-no-kami|lt_ja=比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himemiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Take-Mikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|lt_ja=武甕槌大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-Mikazuchi|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|Minato Ward, Tokyo|ja|港区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Ward, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|ja|中原中也|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūya Nakahara|qq=}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Muro Saisei|ja|室生犀星|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muro Saisei|qq=}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|ja|永仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Einin|qq=}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikko-ikki|ja|一向一揆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikko-ikki|qq=}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haiden|qq=}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkei|qq=}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengū Shrine|qq=}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Emperor|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }}
[[ja:金沢五社]] [[sn:Kanazawa Gosha]]

'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazawa City|qq=}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|qq=}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|ja|前田利家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeda Toshiie|qq=}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine|ja|尾山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|lt=Takami Musubi|lt_ja=高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|qq=}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manor|ja|荘園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manor|qq=}}) of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Taisha|qq=}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Himemiko|ja|比売神|lt=Hime-no-kami|lt_ja=比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himemiko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Take-Mikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|lt_ja=武甕槌大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-Mikazuchi|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|Minato Ward, Tokyo|ja|港区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Ward, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|ja|中原中也|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūya Nakahara|qq=}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Muro Saisei|ja|室生犀星|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muro Saisei|qq=}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|ja|永仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Einin|qq=}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikko-ikki|ja|一向一揆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikko-ikki|qq=}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haiden|qq=}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkei|qq=}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengū Shrine|qq=}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Ojin Emperor|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojin Emperor|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

revision.text:
'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] and [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|qq=}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}''' was founded in 718 ([[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 2). In 1599 ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4) it secretly enshrined [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|Oyama Shrine]], the original enshrined deity, [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami Musubi]], was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ([[:en:Manorialism|Manor]]) of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]]<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in [[:en:港区|Minato Ward, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Kinenadahime no Mikoto]].
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

[[ja:金沢五社]] [[sn:Kanazawa Gosha]]

'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] and [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|qq=}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}''' was founded in 718 ([[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 2). In 1599 ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4) it secretly enshrined [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|Oyama Shrine]], the original enshrined deity, [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami Musubi]], was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ([[:en:Manorialism|Manor]]) of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]]<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].

It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].

The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in [[:en:港区|Minato Ward, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Kinenadahime no Mikoto]].
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

[[ja:金沢五社]] [[sn:Kanazawa Gosha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:日本の名数5]]

'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] and [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|qq=}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}''' was founded in 718 ([[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 2). In 1599 ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4) it secretly enshrined [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|Oyama Shrine]], the original enshrined deity, [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami Musubi]], was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ([[:en:Manorialism|Manor]]) of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]]<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in [[:en:港区|Minato Ward, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].

== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in [[:en:港区|Minato Ward, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Kinenadahime no Mikoto]].
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Buildings and structures in Kanazawa, Ishikawa
revision.text:
'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] and [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|qq=}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|qq=}}''' was founded in 718 ([[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 2). In 1599 ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4) it secretly enshrined [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|Oyama Shrine]], the original enshrined deity, [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami Musubi]], was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ([[:en:Manorialism|Manor]]) of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]]<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in [[:en:港区|Minato Ward, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].

== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ([[:en:Manorialism|Manor]]) of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]]<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|qq=}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in [[:en:港区|Minato Ward, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|qq=}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|qq=}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|qq=}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Kinenadahime no Mikoto]].
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0324/ Utasu Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Shrine Association|qq=}} * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0107/ Kosaka Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Shrine Association|qq=}} * [https://www.shinmeiguu-kanazawa.jp/ Shinmeigu Shrine] * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0121/ Tsubakihara Tenmangu] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Shrine Association|qq=}} * [http://www.yasue-hachimangu.or.jp/ Yasue Hachimangu / Kanazawa Suitengū]
