<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647381]] Wikidata name: Five Shrines of Kanazawa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647381]] Wikidata name: Five Shrines of Kanazawa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] and [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ([[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 2). In 1599 ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4) it secretly enshrined [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|Oyama Shrine]], the original enshrined deity, [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami Musubi]], was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ([[:en:Manorialism|Manor]]) of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]]<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in [[:en:港区|Minato Ward, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Kinenadahime no Mikoto]].
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Kinenadahime no Mikoto]].
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

[[Category:Buildings and structures in Kanazawa, Ishikawa]] [[Category:History of Kanazawa]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11647381}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647381]] Wikidata name: Five Shrines of Kanazawa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647381]] Wikidata name: Five Shrines of Kanazawa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] and [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ([[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 2). In 1599 ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4) it secretly enshrined [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|Oyama Shrine]], the original enshrined deity, [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami Musubi]], was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ([[:en:Manorialism|Manor]]) of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]]<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in [[:en:港区|Minato Ward, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Kinenadahime no Mikoto]].
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647381]] Wikidata name: Five Shrines of Kanazawa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647381]] Wikidata name: Five Shrines of Kanazawa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] and [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ([[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 2). In 1599 ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4) it secretly enshrined [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|Oyama Shrine]], the original enshrined deity, [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami Musubi]], was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ([[:en:Manorialism|Manor]]) of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]]<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, [[:en:Himegami|Hime-no-kami]], and [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami]].
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in [[:en:港区|Minato Ward, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Kinenadahime no Mikoto]].
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by [[:en:Chūya Nakahara|Chūya Nakahara]] was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of [[:en:Murō Saisei|Muro Saisei]]—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ([[:en:Einin|Einin]] 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]]. It was relocated to its present site during the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikko-ikki]], still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]].
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Kinenadahime no Mikoto]].
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647381]] Wikidata name: Five Shrines of Kanazawa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647381]] Wikidata name: Five Shrines of Kanazawa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|en|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|WD=Q998709|lt=Oyama Shrine}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takami Musubi}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manorialism|en|Manorialism|WD=Q1550557|lt=Manor}}) of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-no-kami}}, and {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato Ward, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|en|Chūya Nakahara|WD=Q278215|lt=Chūya Nakahara}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Murō Saisei|en|Murō Saisei|WD=Q331339|lt=Muro Saisei}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Choraku-ji Tenmangū}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikkō-ikki|en|Ikkō-ikki|WD=Q947533|lt=Ikko-ikki}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-no-kami}}, and {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato Ward, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Choraku-ji Tenmangū}}. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

[[ja:金沢五社]]
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11647381}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kanazawa, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Kanazawa]] [[Category:Japanese Enumerations of 5]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]]
'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|en|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|WD=Q998709|lt=Oyama Shrine}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takami Musubi}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manorialism|en|Manorialism|WD=Q1550557|lt=Manor}}) of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-no-kami}}, and {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato Ward, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|en|Chūya Nakahara|WD=Q278215|lt=Chūya Nakahara}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Murō Saisei|en|Murō Saisei|WD=Q331339|lt=Muro Saisei}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Choraku-ji Tenmangū}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikkō-ikki|en|Ikkō-ikki|WD=Q947533|lt=Ikko-ikki}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0324/ Utasu Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0107/ Kosaka Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [https://www.shinmeiguu-kanazawa.jp/ Shinmeigu Shrine] * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0121/ Tsubakihara Tenmangu] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [http://www.yasue-hachimangu.or.jp/ Yasue Hachimangu / Kanazawa Suitengū]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]]
'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|en|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|WD=Q998709|lt=Oyama Shrine}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takami Musubi}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manorialism|en|Manorialism|WD=Q1550557|lt=Manor}}) of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-no-kami}}, and {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato Ward, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|en|Chūya Nakahara|WD=Q278215|lt=Chūya Nakahara}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Murō Saisei|en|Murō Saisei|WD=Q331339|lt=Muro Saisei}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Choraku-ji Tenmangū}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikkō-ikki|en|Ikkō-ikki|WD=Q947533|lt=Ikko-ikki}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|en|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|WD=Q998709|lt=Oyama Shrine}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takami Musubi}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manorialism|en|Manorialism|WD=Q1550557|lt=Manor}}) of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-no-kami}}, and {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato Ward, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|en|Chūya Nakahara|WD=Q278215|lt=Chūya Nakahara}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Murō Saisei|en|Murō Saisei|WD=Q331339|lt=Muro Saisei}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Choraku-ji Tenmangū}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikkō-ikki|en|Ikkō-ikki|WD=Q947533|lt=Ikko-ikki}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0324/ Utasu Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0107/ Kosaka Shrine] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [https://www.shinmeiguu-kanazawa.jp/ Shinmeigu Shrine] * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0121/ Tsubakihara Tenmangu] - {{ill|Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja|石川県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Shrine Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [http://www.yasue-hachimangu.or.jp/ Yasue Hachimangu / Kanazawa Suitengū]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]]
'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City|1=Kanazawa}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie|1=Maeda Toshiie}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|en|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|WD=Q998709|lt=Oyama Shrine|1=Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takami Musubi|1=Takamimusubi}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manorialism|en|Manorialism|WD=Q1550557|lt=Manor|1=Manorialism}}) of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-no-kami|1=Himegami}}, and {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|1=Takemikazuchi}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Dai-jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato Ward, Tokyo|1=港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|en|Chūya Nakahara|WD=Q278215|lt=Chūya Nakahara|1=Chūya Nakahara}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Murō Saisei|en|Murō Saisei|WD=Q331339|lt=Muro Saisei|1=Murō Saisei}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin|1=Einin}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Choraku-ji Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikkō-ikki|en|Ikkō-ikki|WD=Q947533|lt=Ikko-ikki|1=Ikkō-ikki}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei|1=Tengyō}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine|1=Suitengū}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Dai-jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato Ward, Tokyo|1=港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|en|Chūya Nakahara|WD=Q278215|lt=Chūya Nakahara|1=Chūya Nakahara}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Murō Saisei|en|Murō Saisei|WD=Q331339|lt=Muro Saisei|1=Murō Saisei}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin|1=Einin}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Choraku-ji Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikkō-ikki|en|Ikkō-ikki|WD=Q947533|lt=Ikko-ikki|1=Ikkō-ikki}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei|1=Tengyō}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine|1=Suitengū}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11647381}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kanazawa, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Kanazawa]] [[Category:Japanese Enumerations of 5]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金沢五社]]
'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City|1=Kanazawa}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie|1=Maeda Toshiie}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|en|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|WD=Q998709|lt=Oyama Shrine|1=Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takami Musubi|1=Takamimusubi}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manorialism|en|Manorialism|WD=Q1550557|lt=Manor|1=Manorialism}}) of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-no-kami|1=Himegami}}, and {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|1=Takemikazuchi}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1

It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikkō-ikki|en|Ikkō-ikki|qid=Q947533|lt=Ikko-ikki|1=Ikkō-ikki}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|qid=Q3125745|lt=Haiden|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei|1=Tengyō}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|qid=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine|1=Suitengū}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27

revision.text:
'''Kanazawa Gosha''' (金沢五社) is the collective term for the five Yoshida Shinto shrines located in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|qid=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City|1=Kanazawa}}, namely, {{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125927189}}, Kosaka Shrine, Shinmeigu Shrine, Tsubakihara Tenmangu, and Yasue Hachimangu of Yoshida Shinto.
All five shrines were Ranked as prefectural shrines under the modern {{ill|Shrine Ranking system|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Ranking system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|qid=Q10444029}} system. Visiting all five shrines was known as "Gosha-mairi" and was widely practiced.
== Utasu Shrine (宇多須神社) == [[File:Utasu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Utasu Shrine]] '''{{ill|Utasu Shrine|ja|宇多須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utasu Shrine|1=Utasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125927189}}''' was founded in 718 ({{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 2). In 1599 ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 4) it secretly enshrined {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|qid=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie|1=Maeda Toshiie}}, with retainers contributing their ritual fees according to their stipends. With the founding of {{ill|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|en|Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)|qid=Q998709|lt=Oyama Shrine|1=Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)}}, the original enshrined deity, {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|lt=Takami Musubi|1=Takamimusubi}}, was transferred.
Location: Kanazawa City Higashiyama 1-chōme 30-8
== Kosaka Shrine (小坂神社) == [[File:Kosakajinja.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Kosaka Shrine]] '''Kosaka Shrine''' was founded in 717 (the first year of the Yōrō era) on land that had once been a manor ({{ill|Manorialism|en|Manorialism|qid=Q1550557|lt=Manor|1=Manorialism}}) of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|qid=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}}<ref>[https://www4.city.kanazawa.lg.jp/11104/bunkazaimain/shiteibunkazai/yukeikenzou/kosakajinnjya.html Kosaka Shrine Main Hall] - Kanazawa City</ref>. This shrine is also a branch shrine of Kasuga Taisha, affectionately known as "Kasuga-san". The main deities are {{ill|Ameno-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2564508}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|qid=Q22070227|lt=Hime-no-kami|1=Himegami}}, and {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690|lt=Take Mikazuchi-no-ōkami|1=Takemikazuchi}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Yamanoue-chō 42-1
== Shinmeigu Shrine (神明宮) == '''Shinmeigu Shrine''' is one of Japan's Seven Shinmei Shrines, alongside {{ill|Shiba Taian Shrine|ja|芝大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Taian Shrine|1=Shiba Dai-jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11614619}} (Shiba Shinmeigu) in {{ill|港区|en|港区|qid=Q14219833|lt=Minato Ward, Tokyo|1=港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, and Higashi-Iwakura Shinmei (in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q740246}}), among others. It is renowned for the "Aburimochi" ritual held in spring and autumn<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000014703|title = あぶりもちで無病息災 金沢・神明宮で神事|publisher = 北陸新幹線で行こう!北陸・信越観光ナビ（北國新聞）|date = 2018-05-16|accessdate = 2019-10-12}}</ref>. The poem "Circus" by {{ill|Chūya Nakahara|en|Chūya Nakahara|qid=Q278215|lt=Chūya Nakahara|1=Chūya Nakahara}} was inspired by a circus he saw in the shrine's precincts during his childhood, and the shrine—located near the birthplace of {{ill|Murō Saisei|en|Murō Saisei|qid=Q331339|lt=Muro Saisei|1=Murō Saisei}}—has long been associated with poets. The main deities are {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|lt_ja=天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Nomachi 2-chōme 1-8
== Tsubakihara Tenmangu (椿原天満宮) == [[File:Tsubakiharatenmanguu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Tsubakihara Tenmangu]] '''Tsubakihara Tenmangu''' was founded in 1297 ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|qid=Q1327307|lt=Einin|1=Einin}} 5) by the guardian {{ill|Togashi Yoshichika|ja|富樫義親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Yoshichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as one of the {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Choraku-ji Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}}. It was relocated to its present site during the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} period of the Edo period. The site of Tsubakihara Fort, occupied by Susaki Hyō during the {{ill|Ikkō-ikki|en|Ikkō-ikki|qid=Q947533|lt=Ikko-ikki|1=Ikkō-ikki}}, still remains. Nowadays, concerts—including chamber music, folk, rock, blues, and band performances—are frequently held in the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|qid=Q3125745|lt=Haiden|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} to great acclaim. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Tenjinchō 1-chōme 1-13
== Yasue Hachimangu (安江八幡宮) == [[File:Torii in front of Haiden of Yasue Hachiman Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Yasue Hachimangu]] '''Yasue Hachimangu''' was founded in 939 ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei|1=Tengyō}} 2). It is also known as '''Kanazawa Suitengū''', and is the only {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|qid=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine|1=Suitengū}} enshrined in Ishikawa Prefecture. [[File:Stone lantern and sculpture of Kaga-Hachiman Roly-poly doll in Yasue Hachiman Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The statue of {{ill|Kaga Hachimakiagari|ja|加賀八幡起上り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Hachimakiagari|1=Kaga Hachiman okiagari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28689012}} at Yasue Hachimangu]] The local toy "Kaga Hachimakiagari" originated from being dedicated to this shrine, and Ishikawa Prefecture's mascot character, "Hyakumansan", is based on it. The main deities are {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Hotakeda-wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Kinenadahime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}.
Location: Kanazawa City Konohanamachi 11-27
